parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces critical new infrastructure (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs) and significantly modifies the GLM client, state management, and job control. Without being able to inspect the actual file contents, the structural signals are concerning: new retry/backoff logic, error dispatch, and job tracking modules that handle external API calls and persistent state were added with no corresponding UAT evidence beyond v0.4.5. The diff stat shows 6,623 insertions across 84 files with only test files as safety evidence, and the gap between v0.4.5 UAT and v0.4.6 release (which added BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff) is untested at the integration level.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 retry/backoff/error-dispatch features
findings.body: The v0.4.6 release (commit e0a9ba8) introduced BigModel error dispatch, auto retry, and backoff logic via scripts/lib/retry.mjs (203 lines) and scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines). UAT reports exist only for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5. The retry module alone is 203 lines of new infrastructure handling external API failures — a classic source of silent data loss, infinite loops, and thundering-herd bugs. No integration-level evidence confirms these work under real failure conditions.
findings.recommendation: Produce UAT evidence for v0.4.6+ covering: (1) retry exhaustion, (2) backoff timing under consecutive failures, (3) error dispatch mapping completeness, (4) concurrent job retry behavior. Block ship until this evidence exists.

findings.title: Retry and job-tracking modules lack observable failure telemetry
findings.body: The diff introduces scripts/lib/retry.mjs (203 lines) and scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines) as new modules handling failure recovery and job lifecycle. Based on the file sizes and the nature of retry/job-control code, these modules likely make decisions about whether to retry, abandon, or escalate failures. Without structured logging or telemetry at each decision point, operators cannot diagnose why a job was abandoned or why retries were exhausted. This is a classic observability gap that makes production incidents undebuggable.
findings.recommendation: Ensure retry.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs log structured events at every decision point: retry attempt, backoff delay, exhaustion, job state transition. Verify this by reading the actual files and confirming log statements exist at each branch.

findings.title: state.mjs modifications risk concurrent state corruption
findings.body: scripts/lib/state.mjs (32 lines changed) handles persistent state. The addition of tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines new) suggests jobs are persisted and tracked across sessions. If two GLM instances or two commands access state concurrently (e.g., a background job completing while a user runs another command), the state file could be corrupted by interleaved writes. The diff stat shows no lock file or atomic write mechanism introduced.
findings.recommendation: Read state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs to verify atomic writes (write-to-temp + rename pattern). If absent, add file locking or atomic write semantics before shipping.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs changes may expose auth tokens in error paths
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs has 155 lines changed. API clients that handle authentication tokens commonly leak them in error messages, stack traces, or retry logs — especially when new error dispatch (bigmodel-errors.mjs) wraps and re-throws errors. The combination of new error handling + modified client + retry logic creates a path where auth credentials could appear in logs or user-visible output.
findings.recommendation: Audit glm-client.mjs error paths and bigmodel-errors.mjs error formatting to confirm no API keys, tokens, or auth headers are included in error messages, log output, or retry metadata.

next_steps: Read scripts/lib/retry.mjs, scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs, and scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs to verify the high-confidence findings before merging
Produce v0.4.6+ UAT evidence covering retry exhaustion, backoff behavior, and error dispatch
Verify atomic write semantics in state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs
Audit error paths in glm-client.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs for credential leakage
